name: delegate
description:
Delegate a task to a different identity tier.

Use this to assign work to a tier whose specialty matches the task. Provide a clear task description so the receiving tier has full context.

Semantics:
- Creates a FRESH child context loaded with the target tier's full system prompt (identity + constitution + tools).
- Child context does NOT inherit the parent's conversation history.
- Filesystem state IS shared via the parent's worktree (no isolation).
- The child's final result is returned to the lead context as a `[DELEGATION COMPLETE: <tier>] <summary>` user message for re-synthesis.
- If the active tier has `relay_single_delegate` configured, the child's result is auto-relayed to the parent without re-synthesis. The child can also signal `coverage_gap=true` via entropic.complete to SUPPRESS the auto-relay (the lead then receives a [COVERAGE GAP] message instead, with `gap_description` and `suggested_files`, and may chain to a follow-up specialist). #10 v2.1.4.

The available tiers are listed in the `target` enum. Choose based on the tier whose specialty matches the work that needs to be done.

target.description: Target identity tier to delegate to

task.description: Natural language description of the task to delegate

max_turns.description: Maximum number of turns the delegate can use (optional)
